mechanism_narrative: A near-zero federal funds rate (0.19%) signals an aggressively accommodative monetary policy that compresses discount rates and supports equity valuations. Over a 20-day horizon, this persistent accommodation drives positive S&P 500 returns as the market prices in sustained low rates.
regime_hint: monetary easing at zero lower bound
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day returns are negative in >50% of periods when FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%, or Rank IC over any 2-year rolling window is not significantly positive.

mechanism_narrative: A high unemployment rate (9.6%) represents substantial labor market slack. If UNRATE declines by at least 0.5 percentage points over a 3-month period, it indicates improving economic conditions without wage inflation pressure, which supports a positive S&P 500 return over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Periods with UNRATE declines >0.5pp in 3 months do not produce median 60-day S&P 500 returns above zero, or the signal fails to outperform the unconditional median.

mechanism_narrative: The joint condition of a near-zero federal funds rate and rising industrial production signals that monetary accommodation is translating into real economic activity. When FEDFUNDS is below 0.5% and INDPRO shows positive month-over-month growth, the market prices in a self-reinforcing expansion cycle, delivering positive 20-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The joint condition yields median 20-day returns not significantly different from zero, or the interaction term in a regression of returns on FEDFUNDS and INDPRO has a p-value > 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-financial-crisis recovery regime, strong consumer demand as proxied by RSAFS signals that the expansion is durable. When RSAFS is growing at a 3-month annualized rate above 4%, it indicates robust consumer spending that will lift corporate earnings and support S&P 500 returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: Periods with RSAFS growth >4% show median 20-day S&P 500 returns below -1% or the hit rate is not above 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) captures real supply-side activity. A sustained level above 93 (as observed) suggests the manufacturing sector is expanding. When INDPRO is above its 12-month moving average, it signals underlying economic strength that translates into positive weekly S&P 500 returns as earnings expectations improve.
failure_condition: Average 5-day S&P 500 return when INDPRO is above its 12-month MA is not positive, or the frequency of positive returns in such periods is below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) are a leading indicator of residential investment and consumer confidence. When HOUST rises above 600,000 units (seasonally adjusted annual rate) after being below that level, it signals a turning point in the housing market that typically precedes positive equity returns over a 5-day horizon as sentiment improves.
failure_condition: The average 5-day S&P 500 return in the 10 trading days following a HOUST cross above 600 is negative or not statistically different from zero (t-test p>0.05).

mechanism_narrative: The combination of a declining unemployment rate and rising retail sales indicates that labor market improvement is translating into consumer spending. When UNRATE falls by 0.3pp or more over 3 months and RSAFS shows positive year-over-year growth simultaneously, the labor-demand link reinforces economic expansion and supports S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls >0.3pp in 3 months AND RSAFS YoY growth > 0
regime_hint: virtuous cycle of labor and consumption
failure_condition: The joint condition yields a median 60-day S&P 500 return less than or equal to the unconditional median, or the interaction in a linear model has negligible incremental predictive power (ΔR² < 0.01).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) below its recent trend signals a potential manufacturing slowdown. When INDPRO falls below 92 (a threshold near its current level), it indicates supply-side weakness that may presage a broader economic contraction, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over a one-week horizon as investors reassess growth expectations.
failure_condition: Average 5-day S&P 500 return is positive or not statistically different from zero when INDPRO < 92, or the incidence of negative returns is less than 55%.
